title: Great White Sharks in Trouble from Hot Water
summary: Great white sharks are having a hard time because the ocean is getting warmer. These big sharks keep their bodies warmer than the water around them, which helps them swim fast and hunt. But as the water heats up from climate change, they can get too hot and might not survive. They also have less food to eat because of overfishing. Scientists studied sharks and found they need to move to cooler waters or slow down to stay safe. Sharks are important for the ocean because they help keep other animals in balance. If sharks disappear, it could hurt the whole ocean ecosystem. People are working to protect sharks by learning more about them and creating safe areas.

options: Because the ocean is warming from climate change
Because they live in cold water
questions.correct_answer: Because the ocean is warming from climate change

questions.question: Who studied the sharks to learn about their heat?

background_read: Great white sharks are large fish that live in oceans around the world. They are known as apex predators, meaning they are at the top of the food chain and help control other animal populations. Climate change is making Earth warmer, which affects oceans and animals like sharks. Overfishing happens when people catch too many fish, reducing food for sharks. Scientists use tools like sensors to study how sharks handle heat and what we can do to protect them.
Article_Structure: WHO: Great white sharks and scientists studying them
WHAT: Sharks are overheating due to warming oceans and have less food
WHERE: In oceans worldwide, like South Africa
WHY: To explain how climate change and overfishing threaten sharks and why protecting them matters

perspectives.description: Scientists warn that warming oceans and overfishing put sharks at risk of overheating and starvation.
